{"returncode":0,"stdout":""""
Indian News Dataset Scraper
============================
Collects articles from Times of India, Hindustan Times, NDTV, The Hindu
via RSS feeds + full content extraction using trafilatura + BeautifulSoup fallbacks.

Fields collected per article:
  - title, author, content, topics, date, source, url, uri

Usage:
    python scraper.py                         # Run all feeds, save to dataset.json
    python scraper.py --source toi            # Only Times of India
    python scraper.py --category sports       # Only sports feeds
    python scraper.py --limit 50              # Max 50 articles per feed
    python scraper.py --author-url <url>      # Scrape a specific author page
"""

import feedparser
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import trafilatura
import json
import time
import random
import argparse
import re
import logging
from datetime import datetime
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from pathlib import Path

# ─── Logging ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
logging.basicConfig(
    level=logging.INFO,
    format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s",
    datefmt="%H:%M:%S"
)
log = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# ─── Configuration ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

HEADERS = {
    "User-Agent": (
        "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) "
        "AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) "
        "Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36"
    ),
    "Accept-Language": "en-US,en;q=0.9",
    "Accept": "text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8",
}

# Minimum characters for content to be considered valid (paywall detection)
MIN_CONTENT_LENGTH = 400

# Delay between requests (seconds) — be respectful
REQUEST_DELAY_MIN = 3.0
REQUEST_DELAY_MAX = 7.0

# ─── RSS Feed Registry ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

RSS_FEEDS = {
    "toi": {
        "source_name": "Times of India",
        "feeds": {
            "india":       "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/-2128936835.cms",
            "world":       "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/296589292.cms",
            "sports":      "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/4719148.cms",
            "business":    "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/1898055.cms",
            "technology":  "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/66949542.cms",
            "cricket":     "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/54829575.cms",
            "entertainment":"https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/1081479906.cms",
        }
    },
    "ht": {
        "source_name": "Hindustan Times",
        "feeds": {
            "india":       "https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/india-news/rssfeed.xml",
            "world":       "https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/world-news/rssfeed.xml",
            "sports":      "https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/sports/rssfeed.xml",
            "business":    "https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/business/rssfeed.xml",
            "technology":  "https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/technology/rssfeed.xml",
            "cricket":     "https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/cricket/rssfeed.xml",
            "entertainment":"https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/entertainment/rssfeed.xml",
        }
    },
    "ndtv": {
        "source_name": "NDTV",
        "feeds": {
            "india":       "https://feeds.feedburner.com/ndtvnews-india-news",
            "world":       "https://feeds.feedburner.com/ndtvnews-world-news",
            "sports":      "https://feeds.feedburner.com/ndtvnews-sports",
            "business":    "https://feeds.feedburner.com/ndtvnews-business",
            "technology":  "https://feeds.feedburner.com/ndtvnews-tech",
            "cricket":     "https://feeds.feedburner.com/CricketNDTV",
        }
    },
    "hindu": {
        "source_name": "The Hindu",
        "feeds": {
            "india":       "https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/?service=rss",
            "world":       "https://www.thehindu.com/news/international/?service=rss",
            "sports":      "https://www.thehindu.com/sport/?service=rss",
            "business":    "https://www.thehindu.com/business/?service=rss",
            "technology":  "https://www.thehindu.com/sci-tech/?service=rss",
            "cricket":     "https://www.thehindu.com/sport/cricket/?service=rss",
        }
    },
    "indiatoday": {
        "source_name": "India Today",
        "feeds": {
            "india":       "https://www.indiatoday.in/rss/1206578",
            "sports":      "https://www.indiatoday.in/rss/1206579",
            "business":    "https://www.indiatoday.in/rss/1206580",
            "technology":  "https://www.indiatoday.in/rss/1206581",
        }
    },
    "cricbuzz": {
        "source_name": "Cricbuzz",
        "feeds": {
            "cricket":     "https://www.cricbuzz.com/cricket-news/rss-feed",
            "india":       "https://www.cricbuzz.com/cricket-news/rss-feed?tag=India",
        }
    },
}

# ─── Author Extraction: Site-Specific Selectors ───────────────────────────────
#
# Each site uses different HTML patterns for author bylines.
# We try multiple selectors in order and return the first match.
#
AUTHOR_SELECTORS = {
    "indiatimes.com": [
        # Try structured data first (most reliable)
        {"type": "meta",   "attr": "name",       "value": "author"},
        {"type": "meta",   "attr": "property",   "value": "article:author"},
        # JSON-LD structured data
        {"type": "jsonld", "key": "author"},
        # HTML class-based selectors (TOI uses these)
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "div.auth-nm.byline"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "span.auth-nm"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "a.author"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[class*="author"]'},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[rel="author"]'},
    ],
    "hindustantimes.com": [
        {"type": "meta",   "attr": "name",       "value": "author"},
        {"type": "jsonld", "key": "author"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "span.author-name"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "div.author-name"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[itemprop="author"]'},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[class*="authorName"]'},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "a.storyBy"},
    ],
    "ndtv.com": [
        {"type": "meta",   "attr": "name",       "value": "author"},
        {"type": "jsonld", "key": "author"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "span.author-name"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "div.author_name"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[itemprop="author"]'},
    ],
    "thehindu.com": [
        {"type": "meta",   "attr": "name",       "value": "author"},
        {"type": "jsonld", "key": "author"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "span.author-name"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "a.auth-nm"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[rel="author"]'},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[class*="author"]'},
    ],
    "indiatoday.in": [
        {"type": "meta",   "attr": "name",       "value": "author"},
        {"type": "jsonld", "key": "author"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "span.jsx-author-name"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[class*="authorName"]'},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[itemprop="author"]'},
    ],
    "cricbuzz.com": [
        {"type": "meta",   "attr": "name",       "value": "author"},
        {"type": "jsonld", "key": "author"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "div.cb-author"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[itemprop="author"]'},
    ],
    # Generic fallback for any other domain
    "_default": [
        {"type": "meta",   "attr": "name",       "value": "author"},
        {"type": "meta",   "attr": "property",   "value": "article:author"},
        {"type": "jsonld", "key": "author"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[rel="author"]'},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[itemprop="author"]'},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[class*="author"]'},
    ],
}

# ─── Helper: Identify domain ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

def get_domain(url: str) -> str:
    parsed = urlparse(url)
    host = parsed.netloc.lower()
    for key in AUTHOR_SELECTORS:
        if key in host:
            return key
    return "_default"

# ─── Author Extraction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def extract_author(soup: BeautifulSoup, url: str) -> str:
    domain = get_domain(url)
    selectors = AUTHOR_SELECTORS.get(domain, AUTHOR_SELECTORS["_default"])

    for sel in selectors:
        try:
            result = None

            if sel["type"] == "meta":
                tag = soup.find("meta", attrs={sel["attr"]: sel["value"]})
                if tag and tag.get("content"):
                    result = tag["content"].strip()

            elif sel["type"] == "jsonld":
                # Parse JSON-LD structured data blocks
                for script in soup.find_all("script", type="application/ld+json"):
                    try:
                        data = json.loads(script.string or "")
                        # Handle both single object and array
                        items = data if isinstance(data, list) else [data]
                        for item in items:
                            author = item.get(sel["key"])
                            if isinstance(author, str) and author:
                                result = author
                                break
                            elif isinstance(author, dict):
                                result = author.get("name", "")
                                break
                            elif isinstance(author, list) and author:
                                first = author[0]
                                result = first.get("name", "") if isinstance(first, dict) else str(first)
                                break
                        if result:
                            break
                    except (json.JSONDecodeError, AttributeError):
                        continue

            elif sel["type"] == "css":
                tag = soup.select_one(sel["selector"])
                if tag:
                    result = tag.get_text(separator=" ", strip=True)

            # Clean and validate result
            if result:
                # Remove common noise
                result = re.sub(r"^(by|written by|author:)\s*", "", result, flags=re.IGNORECASE).strip()
                # Reject if it looks like garbage (too long, has HTML, etc.)
                if 2 < len(result) < 100 and "<" not in result:
                    return result

        except Exception:
            continue

    return "Unknown"

# ─── Category Extraction ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def extract_category_from_url(url: str) -> str:
    """Infer category from URL path segments."""
    path = urlparse(url).path.lower()
    category_keywords = {
        "cricket":       ["cricket"],
        "sports":        ["sport", "ipl", "football", "tennis"],
        "technology":    ["tech", "gadget", "science", "sci-tech"],
        "business":      ["business", "economy", "finance", "market"],
        "entertainment": ["entertainment", "bollywood", "movie", "film", "tv"],
        "india":         ["india", "national"],
        "world":         ["world", "international", "global"],
        "politics":      ["politics", "election"],
    }
    for category, keywords in category_keywords.items():
        if any(kw in path for kw in keywords):
            return category
    return "general"

def extract_topics(soup: BeautifulSoup, url: str, rss_tags: list = None) -> list:
    """Extract topics/tags from multiple sources."""
    topics = set()

    # From RSS entry tags if provided
    if rss_tags:
        for tag in rss_tags:
            if isinstance(tag, dict):
                topics.add(tag.get("term", ""))
            elif isinstance(tag, str):
                topics.add(tag)

    # From meta keywords
    meta_kw = soup.find("meta", attrs={"name": "keywords"})
    if meta_kw and meta_kw.get("content"):
        for kw in meta_kw["content"].split(","):
            kw = kw.strip()
            if kw:
                topics.add(kw)

    # From JSON-LD keywords
    for script in soup.find_all("script", type="application/ld+json"):
        try:
            data = json.loads(script.string or "")
            items = data if isinstance(data, list) else [data]
            for item in items:
                keywords = item.get("keywords", [])
                if isinstance(keywords, str):
                    for k in keywords.split(","):
                        topics.add(k.strip())
                elif isinstance(keywords, list):
                    topics.update([k.strip() for k in keywords if k])
        except (json.JSONDecodeError, AttributeError):
            continue

    # URL-based category as fallback
    url_category = extract_category_from_url(url)
    if url_category != "general":
        topics.add(url_category)

    # Clean and deduplicate
    cleaned = [t for t in topics if t and len(t) > 1]
    return sorted(set(cleaned))[:15]  # Cap at 15 topics

# ─── Content Extraction (trafilatura with fallback) ───────────────────────────

def extract_content(html: str, url: str) -> str:
    """Extract article body using trafilatura with BeautifulSoup fallback."""
    # Primary: trafilatura (best for news articles)
    content = trafilatura.extract(
        html,
        include_comments=False,
        include_tables=False,
        no_fallback=False,
        favor_precision=True,
    )
    if content and len(content) >= MIN_CONTENT_LENGTH:
        return content.strip()

    # Fallback: BeautifulSoup heuristic
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")
    
    # Remove noise elements
    for tag in soup.find_all(["script", "style", "nav", "footer", "header",
                               "aside", "iframe", "figure", "figcaption",
                               "noscript", "form"]):
        tag.decompose()

    # Try common article body containers
    content_selectors = [
        {"tag": "div", "class_": re.compile(r"article[-_]?(body|content|text)", re.I)},
        {"tag": "div", "class_": re.compile(r"(story|post)[-_]?(body|content|text)", re.I)},
        {"tag": "article"},
        {"tag": "div", "itemprop": "articleBody"},
        {"tag": "div", "class_": re.compile(r"content[-_]?body", re.I)},
    ]

    for sel in content_selectors:
        tag_name = sel.pop("tag")
        elem = soup.find(tag_name, **sel)
        if elem:
            text = elem.get_text(separator="\n", strip=True)
            if len(text) >= MIN_CONTENT_LENGTH:
                return text
        sel["tag"] = tag_name  # restore for next iteration

    # Last resort: all paragraphs
    paragraphs = soup.find_all("p")
    text = "\n".join(p.get_text(strip=True) for p in paragraphs if len(p.get_text(strip=True)) > 50)
    return text if len(text) >= MIN_CONTENT_LENGTH else ""

# ─── Date Extraction ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def extract_date(soup: BeautifulSoup, rss_date: str = None) -> str:
    """Extract publish date with multiple fallbacks."""
    # 1. RSS-provided date
    if rss_date:
        return rss_date

    # 2. Open Graph / meta tags
    for attr, value in [
        ("property", "article:published_time"),
        ("name", "publishdate"),
        ("name", "publish-date"),
        ("name", "date"),
        ("itemprop", "datePublished"),
    ]:
        tag = soup.find("meta", attrs={attr: value})
        if tag and tag.get("content"):
            return tag["content"].strip()

    # 3. JSON-LD
    for script in soup.find_all("script", type="application/ld+json"):
        try:
            data = json.loads(script.string or "")
            items = data if isinstance(data, list) else [data]
            for item in items:
                for key in ["datePublished", "dateCreated", "dateModified"]:
                    if item.get(key):
                        return item[key]
        except (json.JSONDecodeError, AttributeError):
            continue

    # 4. time element
    time_tag = soup.find("time", attrs={"datetime": True})
    if time_tag:
        return time_tag["datetime"]

    return "Unknown"

# ─── URI Generation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def extract_uri(url: str) -> str:
    """Generate a clean, unique URI from the URL."""
    path = urlparse(url).path
    # Remove file extension and trailing slashes
    slug = path.rstrip("/").split("/")[-1]
    slug = re.sub(r"\.(cms|html|htm|aspx|php)$", "", slug)
    return slug if slug else path.replace("/", "-").strip("-")

# ─── Main Article Fetcher ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def fetch_article(url: str, source_name: str, category: str = None,
                  rss_date: str = None, rss_tags: list = None) -> dict | None:
    """
    Fetch and parse a single article URL.
    Returns a dict with all required fields, or None on failure.
    """
    try:
        response = requests.get(url, headers=HEADERS, timeout=12)
        response.raise_for_status()
        html = response.text

        soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")

        # Extract all fields
        title = ""
        og_title = soup.find("meta", property="og:title")
        if og_title and og_title.get("content"):
            title = og_title["content"].strip()
        if not title:
            h1 = soup.find("h1")
            title = h1.get_text(strip=True) if h1 else "Unknown"

        author  = extract_author(soup, url)
        content = extract_content(html, url)
        date    = extract_date(soup, rss_date)
        topics  = extract_topics(soup, url, rss_tags)

        # Paywall / empty content detection
        if not content:
            log.warning(f"  ⚠ No content extracted (possible paywall): {url}")
            return None

        if len(content) < MIN_CONTENT_LENGTH:
            log.warning(f"  ⚠ Content too short ({len(content)} chars), likely truncated: {url}")
            return None

        return {
            "title":   title,
            "author":  author,
            "content": content,
            "topics":  topics,
            "date":    date,
            "source":  source_name,
            "url":     url,
            "uri":     extract_uri(url),
        }

    except requests.exceptions.HTTPError as e:
        log.warning(f"  ✗ HTTP {e.response.status_code}: {url}")
    except requests.exceptions.Timeout:
        log.warning(f"  ✗ Timeout: {url}")
    except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
        log.warning(f"  ✗ Request error: {url} — {e}")
    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"  ✗ Unexpected error: {url} — {e}")
    return None

# ─── RSS Feed Processor ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def process_rss_feed(rss_url: str, source_name: str, category: str,
                     limit: int = 0) -> list:
    """Parse an RSS feed and fetch all articles from it."""
    log.info(f"📡 Fetching RSS: [{source_name}] {category} — {rss_url}")
    
    try:
        feed = feedparser.parse(rss_url)
    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"  ✗ Failed to parse RSS: {e}")
        return []

    entries = feed.entries
    if limit:
        entries = entries[:limit]

    log.info(f"  Found {len(entries)} entries")

    articles = []
    for i, entry in enumerate(entries, 1):
        url = entry.get("link", "")
        if not url:
            continue

        log.info(f"  [{i}/{len(entries)}] {entry.get('title', 'No title')[:60]}")

        # Get RSS-provided metadata
        rss_date = entry.get("published") or entry.get("updated")
        rss_tags = entry.get("tags", [])

        article = fetch_article(
            url=url,
            source_name=source_name,
            category=category,
            rss_date=rss_date,
            rss_tags=rss_tags,
        )

        if article:
            articles.append(article)
            log.info(f"  ✓ Author: {article['author']} | {len(article['content'])} chars")
        
        # Rate limiting
        time.sleep(random.uniform(REQUEST_DELAY_MIN, REQUEST_DELAY_MAX))

    return articles

# ─── Author Page Scraper ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def scrape_author_page(author_url: str, source_name: str, limit: int = 50) -> list:
    """
    Scrape an author page to find all their article URLs, then fetch each.
    Works on TOI and HT author profile pages.
    """
    log.info(f"👤 Scraping author page: {author_url}")
    
    try:
        response = requests.get(author_url, headers=HEADERS, timeout=12)
        response.raise_for_status()
        soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, "html.parser")
    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"  ✗ Failed to fetch author page: {e}")
        return []

    # Find article links — try common patterns
    article_urls = set()
    domain = urlparse(author_url).netloc

    for a_tag in soup.find_all("a", href=True):
        href = a_tag["href"]
        # Reconstruct relative URLs
        if href.startswith("/"):
            href = f"https://{domain}{href}"
        # Filter: must be same domain and look like an article
        if domain in href and re.search(r"(articleshow|article|story|news).*\d{4,}", href):
            article_urls.add(href)

    log.info(f"  Found {len(article_urls)} article links")

    articles = []
    for i, url in enumerate(list(article_urls)[:limit], 1):
        log.info(f"  [{i}] {url[:80]}")
        article = fetch_article(url=url, source_name=source_name)
        if article:
            articles.append(article)
        time.sleep(random.uniform(REQUEST_DELAY_MIN, REQUEST_DELAY_MAX))

    return articles

# ─── Dataset Save ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def save_dataset(articles: list, output_path: str):
    """Save articles to JSON, appending to existing data if file exists."""
    path = Path(output_path)
    
    existing = []
    if path.exists():
        with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            existing = json.load(f)
        log.info(f"  Loaded {len(existing)} existing articles")

    # Deduplicate by URL
    existing_urls = {a["url"] for a in existing}
    new_articles = [a for a in articles if a["url"] not in existing_urls]

    combined = existing + new_articles

    with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        json.dump(combined, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False, default=str)

    log.info(f"💾 Saved {len(new_articles)} new articles (total: {len(combined)}) → {output_path}")
    return len(new_articles)

# ─── Stats Printer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def print_stats(articles: list):
    """Print a summary of collected data quality."""
    if not articles:
        print("\n⚠ No articles collected.")
        return

    authors = [a["author"] for a in articles]
    known = [a for a in authors if a != "Unknown"]
    
    # Author frequency
    from collections import Counter
    author_counts = Counter(known).most_common(10)

    print("\n" + "="*55)
    print(f"📊 COLLECTION SUMMARY")
    print("="*55)
    print(f"  Total articles:     {len(articles)}")
    print(f"  Author found:       {len(known)} ({100*len(known)//len(articles)}%)")
    print(f"  Author unknown:     {len(articles)-len(known)}")
    print(f"  Unique authors:     {len(set(known))}")
    
    sources = Counter(a["source"] for a in articles)
    print(f"\n  By source:")
    for src, count in sources.most_common():
        print(f"    {src:<25} {count}")

    print(f"\n  Top authors:")
    for author, count in author_counts:
        print(f"    {author:<30} {count} articles")
    print("="*55)

# ─── CLI Entry Point ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Indian News Dataset Scraper")
    parser.add_argument("--source",     default="all",    help="Source: all, toi, ht, ndtv, hindu, indiatoday, cricbuzz")
    parser.add_argument("--category",   default="all",    help="Category: all, india, world, sports, cricket, business, technology, entertainment")
    parser.add_argument("--limit",      type=int, default=20, help="Max articles per RSS feed (0 = no limit)")
    parser.add_argument("--output",     default="dataset.json", help="Output JSON file path")
    parser.add_argument("--author-url", default=None,     help="Scrape specific author page URL")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    all_articles = []

    # Author page mode
    if args.author_url:
        source_name = "Custom"
        for src_key, src_data in RSS_FEEDS.items():
            if src_key in args.author_url:
                source_name = src_data["source_name"]
                break
        articles = scrape_author_page(args.author_url, source_name, limit=args.limit or 50)
        all_articles.extend(articles)

    else:
        # RSS feed mode
        sources_to_run = RSS_FEEDS if args.source == "all" else {
            args.source: RSS_FEEDS[args.source]
        } if args.source in RSS_FEEDS else {}

        if not sources_to_run:
            log.error(f"Unknown source: {args.source}. Choose from: {', '.join(RSS_FEEDS.keys())}")
            return

        for src_key, src_data in sources_to_run.items():
            source_name = src_data["source_name"]
            feeds = src_data["feeds"]

            if args.category != "all":
                feeds = {args.category: feeds[args.category]} if args.category in feeds else {}

            for category, rss_url in feeds.items():
                articles = process_rss_feed(
                    rss_url=rss_url,
                    source_name=source_name,
                    category=category,
                    limit=args.limit,
                )
                all_articles.extend(articles)

    print_stats(all_articles)
    save_dataset(all_articles, args.output)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

{"returncode":0,"stdout":""""
Indian News Dataset Scraper
============================
Collects articles from Times of India, Hindustan Times, NDTV, The Hindu
via RSS feeds + full content extraction using trafilatura + BeautifulSoup fallbacks.

Fields collected per article:
  - title, author, content, topics, date, source, url, uri

Usage:
    python scraper.py                         # Run all feeds, save to dataset.json
    python scraper.py --source toi            # Only Times of India
    python scraper.py --category sports       # Only sports feeds
    python scraper.py --limit 50              # Max 50 articles per feed
    python scraper.py --author-url <url>      # Scrape a specific author page
"""

import feedparser
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import trafilatura
import json
import time
import random
import argparse
import re
import logging
from datetime import datetime
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from pathlib import Path

# ─── Logging ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
logging.basicConfig(
    level=logging.INFO,
    format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s",
    datefmt="%H:%M:%S"
)
log = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# ─── Configuration ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

HEADERS = {
    "User-Agent": (
        "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) "
        "AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) "
        "Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36"
    ),
    "Accept-Language": "en-US,en;q=0.9",
    "Accept": "text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8",
}

# Minimum characters for content to be considered valid (paywall detection)
MIN_CONTENT_LENGTH = 400

# Delay between requests (seconds) — be respectful
REQUEST_DELAY_MIN = 3.0
REQUEST_DELAY_MAX = 7.0

# ─── RSS Feed Registry ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

RSS_FEEDS = {
    "toi": {
        "source_name": "Times of India",
        "feeds": {
            "india":       "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/-2128936835.cms",
            "world":       "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/296589292.cms",
            "sports":      "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/4719148.cms",
            "business":    "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/1898055.cms",
            "technology":  "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/66949542.cms",
            "cricket":     "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/54829575.cms",
            "entertainment":"https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/1081479906.cms",
        }
    },
    "ht": {
        "source_name": "Hindustan Times",
        "feeds": {
            "india":       "https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/india-news/rssfeed.xml",
            "world":       "https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/world-news/rssfeed.xml",
            "sports":      "https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/sports/rssfeed.xml",
            "business":    "https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/business/rssfeed.xml",
            "technology":  "https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/technology/rssfeed.xml",
            "cricket":     "https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/cricket/rssfeed.xml",
            "entertainment":"https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/entertainment/rssfeed.xml",
        }
    },
    "ndtv": {
        "source_name": "NDTV",
        "feeds": {
            "india":       "https://feeds.feedburner.com/ndtvnews-india-news",
            "world":       "https://feeds.feedburner.com/ndtvnews-world-news",
            "sports":      "https://feeds.feedburner.com/ndtvnews-sports",
            "business":    "https://feeds.feedburner.com/ndtvnews-business",
            "technology":  "https://feeds.feedburner.com/ndtvnews-tech",
            "cricket":     "https://feeds.feedburner.com/CricketNDTV",
        }
    },
    "hindu": {
        "source_name": "The Hindu",
        "feeds": {
            "india":       "https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/?service=rss",
            "world":       "https://www.thehindu.com/news/international/?service=rss",
            "sports":      "https://www.thehindu.com/sport/?service=rss",
            "business":    "https://www.thehindu.com/business/?service=rss",
            "technology":  "https://www.thehindu.com/sci-tech/?service=rss",
            "cricket":     "https://www.thehindu.com/sport/cricket/?service=rss",
        }
    },
    "indiatoday": {
        "source_name": "India Today",
        "feeds": {
            "india":       "https://www.indiatoday.in/rss/1206578",
            "sports":      "https://www.indiatoday.in/rss/1206579",
            "business":    "https://www.indiatoday.in/rss/1206580",
            "technology":  "https://www.indiatoday.in/rss/1206581",
        }
    },
    "cricbuzz": {
        "source_name": "Cricbuzz",
        "feeds": {
            "cricket":     "https://www.cricbuzz.com/cricket-news/rss-feed",
            "india":       "https://www.cricbuzz.com/cricket-news/rss-feed?tag=India",
        }
    },
}

# ─── Author Extraction: Site-Specific Selectors ───────────────────────────────
#
# Each site uses different HTML patterns for author bylines.
# We try multiple selectors in order and return the first match.
#
AUTHOR_SELECTORS = {
    "indiatimes.com": [
        # Try structured data first (most reliable)
        {"type": "meta",   "attr": "name",       "value": "author"},
        {"type": "meta",   "attr": "property",   "value": "article:author"},
        # JSON-LD structured data
        {"type": "jsonld", "key": "author"},
        # HTML class-based selectors (TOI uses these)
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "div.auth-nm.byline"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "span.auth-nm"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "a.author"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[class*="author"]'},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[rel="author"]'},
    ],
    "hindustantimes.com": [
        {"type": "meta",   "attr": "name",       "value": "author"},
        {"type": "jsonld", "key": "author"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "span.author-name"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "div.author-name"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[itemprop="author"]'},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[class*="authorName"]'},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "a.storyBy"},
    ],
    "ndtv.com": [
        {"type": "meta",   "attr": "name",       "value": "author"},
        {"type": "jsonld", "key": "author"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "span.author-name"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "div.author_name"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[itemprop="author"]'},
    ],
    "thehindu.com": [
        {"type": "meta",   "attr": "name",       "value": "author"},
        {"type": "jsonld", "key": "author"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "span.author-name"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "a.auth-nm"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[rel="author"]'},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[class*="author"]'},
    ],
    "indiatoday.in": [
        {"type": "meta",   "attr": "name",       "value": "author"},
        {"type": "jsonld", "key": "author"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "span.jsx-author-name"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[class*="authorName"]'},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[itemprop="author"]'},
    ],
    "cricbuzz.com": [
        {"type": "meta",   "attr": "name",       "value": "author"},
        {"type": "jsonld", "key": "author"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": "div.cb-author"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[itemprop="author"]'},
    ],
    # Generic fallback for any other domain
    "_default": [
        {"type": "meta",   "attr": "name",       "value": "author"},
        {"type": "meta",   "attr": "property",   "value": "article:author"},
        {"type": "jsonld", "key": "author"},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[rel="author"]'},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[itemprop="author"]'},
        {"type": "css",    "selector": '[class*="author"]'},
    ],
}

# ─── Helper: Identify domain ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

def get_domain(url: str) -> str:
    parsed = urlparse(url)
    host = parsed.netloc.lower()
    for key in AUTHOR_SELECTORS:
        if key in host:
            return key
    return "_default"

# ─── Author Extraction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def extract_author(soup: BeautifulSoup, url: str) -> str:
    domain = get_domain(url)
    selectors = AUTHOR_SELECTORS.get(domain, AUTHOR_SELECTORS["_default"])

    for sel in selectors:
        try:
            result = None

            if sel["type"] == "meta":
                tag = soup.find("meta", attrs={sel["attr"]: sel["value"]})
                if tag and tag.get("content"):
                    result = tag["content"].strip()

            elif sel["type"] == "jsonld":
                # Parse JSON-LD structured data blocks
                for script in soup.find_all("script", type="application/ld+json"):
                    try:
                        data = json.loads(script.string or "")
                        # Handle both single object and array
                        items = data if isinstance(data, list) else [data]
                        for item in items:
                            author = item.get(sel["key"])
                            if isinstance(author, str) and author:
                                result = author
                                break
                            elif isinstance(author, dict):
                                result = author.get("name", "")
                                break
                            elif isinstance(author, list) and author:
                                first = author[0]
                                result = first.get("name", "") if isinstance(first, dict) else str(first)
                                break
                        if result:
                            break
                    except (json.JSONDecodeError, AttributeError):
                        continue

            elif sel["type"] == "css":
                tag = soup.select_one(sel["selector"])
                if tag:
                    result = tag.get_text(separator=" ", strip=True)

            # Clean and validate result
            if result:
                # Remove common noise
                result = re.sub(r"^(by|written by|author:)\s*", "", result, flags=re.IGNORECASE).strip()
                # Reject if it looks like garbage (too long, has HTML, etc.)
                if 2 < len(result) < 100 and "<" not in result:
                    return result

        except Exception:
            continue

    return "Unknown"

# ─── Category Extraction ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def extract_category_from_url(url: str) -> str:
    """Infer category from URL path segments."""
    path = urlparse(url).path.lower()
    category_keywords = {
        "cricket":       ["cricket"],
        "sports":        ["sport", "ipl", "football", "tennis"],
        "technology":    ["tech", "gadget", "science", "sci-tech"],
        "business":      ["business", "economy", "finance", "market"],
        "entertainment": ["entertainment", "bollywood", "movie", "film", "tv"],
        "india":         ["india", "national"],
        "world":         ["world", "international", "global"],
        "politics":      ["politics", "election"],
    }
    for category, keywords in category_keywords.items():
        if any(kw in path for kw in keywords):
            return category
    return "general"

def extract_topics(soup: BeautifulSoup, url: str, rss_tags: list = None) -> list:
    """Extract topics/tags from multiple sources."""
    topics = set()

    # From RSS entry tags if provided
    if rss_tags:
        for tag in rss_tags:
            if isinstance(tag, dict):
                topics.add(tag.get("term", ""))
            elif isinstance(tag, str):
                topics.add(tag)

    # From meta keywords
    meta_kw = soup.find("meta", attrs={"name": "keywords"})
    if meta_kw and meta_kw.get("content"):
        for kw in meta_kw["content"].split(","):
            kw = kw.strip()
            if kw:
                topics.add(kw)

    # From JSON-LD keywords
    for script in soup.find_all("script", type="application/ld+json"):
        try:
            data = json.loads(script.string or "")
            items = data if isinstance(data, list) else [data]
            for item in items:
                keywords = item.get("keywords", [])
                if isinstance(keywords, str):
                    for k in keywords.split(","):
                        topics.add(k.strip())
                elif isinstance(keywords, list):
                    topics.update([k.strip() for k in keywords if k])
        except (json.JSONDecodeError, AttributeError):
            continue

    # URL-based category as fallback
    url_category = extract_category_from_url(url)
    if url_category != "general":
        topics.add(url_category)

    # Clean and deduplicate
    cleaned = [t for t in topics if t and len(t) > 1]
    return sorted(set(cleaned))[:15]  # Cap at 15 topics

# ─── Content Extraction (trafilatura with fallback) ───────────────────────────

def extract_content(html: str, url: str) -> str:
    """Extract article body using trafilatura with BeautifulSoup fallback."""
    # Primary: trafilatura (best for news articles)
    content = trafilatura.extract(
        html,
        include_comments=False,
        include_tables=False,
        no_fallback=False,
        favor_precision=True,
    )
    if content and len(content) >= MIN_CONTENT_LENGTH:
        return content.strip()

    # Fallback: BeautifulSoup heuristic
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")
    
    # Remove noise elements
    for tag in soup.find_all(["script", "style", "nav", "footer", "header",
                               "aside", "iframe", "figure", "figcaption",
                               "noscript", "form"]):
        tag.decompose()

    # Try common article body containers
    content_selectors = [
        {"tag": "div", "class_": re.compile(r"article[-_]?(body|content|text)", re.I)},
        {"tag": "div", "class_": re.compile(r"(story|post)[-_]?(body|content|text)", re.I)},
        {"tag": "article"},
        {"tag": "div", "itemprop": "articleBody"},
        {"tag": "div", "class_": re.compile(r"content[-_]?body", re.I)},
    ]

    for sel in content_selectors:
        tag_name = sel.pop("tag")
        elem = soup.find(tag_name, **sel)
        if elem:
            text = elem.get_text(separator="\n", strip=True)
            if len(text) >= MIN_CONTENT_LENGTH:
                return text
        sel["tag"] = tag_name  # restore for next iteration

    # Last resort: all paragraphs
    paragraphs = soup.find_all("p")
    text = "\n".join(p.get_text(strip=True) for p in paragraphs if len(p.get_text(strip=True)) > 50)
    return text if len(text) >= MIN_CONTENT_LENGTH else ""

# ─── Date Extraction ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def extract_date(soup: BeautifulSoup, rss_date: str = None) -> str:
    """Extract publish date with multiple fallbacks."""
    # 1. RSS-provided date
    if rss_date:
        return rss_date

    # 2. Open Graph / meta tags
    for attr, value in [
        ("property", "article:published_time"),
        ("name", "publishdate"),
        ("name", "publish-date"),
        ("name", "date"),
        ("itemprop", "datePublished"),
    ]:
        tag = soup.find("meta", attrs={attr: value})
        if tag and tag.get("content"):
            return tag["content"].strip()

    # 3. JSON-LD
    for script in soup.find_all("script", type="application/ld+json"):
        try:
            data = json.loads(script.string or "")
            items = data if isinstance(data, list) else [data]
            for item in items:
                for key in ["datePublished", "dateCreated", "dateModified"]:
                    if item.get(key):
                        return item[key]
        except (json.JSONDecodeError, AttributeError):
            continue

    # 4. time element
    time_tag = soup.find("time", attrs={"datetime": True})
    if time_tag:
        return time_tag["datetime"]

    return "Unknown"

# ─── URI Generation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def extract_uri(url: str) -> str:
    """Generate a clean, unique URI from the URL."""
    path = urlparse(url).path
    # Remove file extension and trailing slashes
    slug = path.rstrip("/").split("/")[-1]
    slug = re.sub(r"\.(cms|html|htm|aspx|php)$", "", slug)
    return slug if slug else path.replace("/", "-").strip("-")

# ─── Main Article Fetcher ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def fetch_article(url: str, source_name: str, category: str = None,
                  rss_date: str = None, rss_tags: list = None) -> dict | None:
    """
    Fetch and parse a single article URL.
    Returns a dict with all required fields, or None on failure.
    """
    try:
        response = requests.get(url, headers=HEADERS, timeout=12)
        response.raise_for_status()
        html = response.text

        soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")

        # Extract all fields
        title = ""
        og_title = soup.find("meta", property="og:title")
        if og_title and og_title.get("content"):
            title = og_title["content"].strip()
        if not title:
            h1 = soup.find("h1")
            title = h1.get_text(strip=True) if h1 else "Unknown"

        author  = extract_author(soup, url)
        content = extract_content(html, url)
        date    = extract_date(soup, rss_date)
        topics  = extract_topics(soup, url, rss_tags)

        # Paywall / empty content detection
        if not content:
            log.warning(f"  ⚠ No content extracted (possible paywall): {url}")
            return None

        if len(content) < MIN_CONTENT_LENGTH:
            log.warning(f"  ⚠ Content too short ({len(content)} chars), likely truncated: {url}")
            return None

        return {
            "title":   title,
            "author":  author,
            "content": content,
            "topics":  topics,
            "date":    date,
            "source":  source_name,
            "url":     url,
            "uri":     extract_uri(url),
        }

    except requests.exceptions.HTTPError as e:
        log.warning(f"  ✗ HTTP {e.response.status_code}: {url}")
    except requests.exceptions.Timeout:
        log.warning(f"  ✗ Timeout: {url}")
    except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
        log.warning(f"  ✗ Request error: {url} — {e}")
    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"  ✗ Unexpected error: {url} — {e}")
    return None

# ─── RSS Feed Processor ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def process_rss_feed(rss_url: str, source_name: str, category: str,
                     limit: int = 0) -> list:
    """Parse an RSS feed and fetch all articles from it."""
    log.info(f"📡 Fetching RSS: [{source_name}] {category} — {rss_url}")
    
    try:
        feed = feedparser.parse(rss_url)
    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"  ✗ Failed to parse RSS: {e}")
        return []

    entries = feed.entries
    if limit:
        entries = entries[:limit]

    log.info(f"  Found {len(entries)} entries")

    articles = []
    for i, entry in enumerate(entries, 1):
        url = entry.get("link", "")
        if not url:
            continue

        log.info(f"  [{i}/{len(entries)}] {entry.get('title', 'No title')[:60]}")

        # Get RSS-provided metadata
        rss_date = entry.get("published") or entry.get("updated")
        rss_tags = entry.get("tags", [])

        article = fetch_article(
            url=url,
            source_name=source_name,
            category=category,
            rss_date=rss_date,
            rss_tags=rss_tags,
        )

        if article:
            articles.append(article)
            log.info(f"  ✓ Author: {article['author']} | {len(article['content'])} chars")
        
        # Rate limiting
        time.sleep(random.uniform(REQUEST_DELAY_MIN, REQUEST_DELAY_MAX))

    return articles

# ─── Author Page Scraper ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def scrape_author_page(author_url: str, source_name: str, limit: int = 50) -> list:
    """
    Scrape an author page to find all their article URLs, then fetch each.
    Works on TOI and HT author profile pages.
    """
    log.info(f"👤 Scraping author page: {author_url}")
    
    try:
        response = requests.get(author_url, headers=HEADERS, timeout=12)
        response.raise_for_status()
        soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, "html.parser")
    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"  ✗ Failed to fetch author page: {e}")
        return []

    # Find article links — try common patterns
    article_urls = set()
    domain = urlparse(author_url).netloc

    for a_tag in soup.find_all("a", href=True):
        href = a_tag["href"]
        # Reconstruct relative URLs
        if href.startswith("/"):
            href = f"https://{domain}{href}"
        # Filter: must be same domain and look like an article
        if domain in href and re.search(r"(articleshow|article|story|news).*\d{4,}", href):
            article_urls.add(href)

    log.info(f"  Found {len(article_urls)} article links")

    articles = []
    for i, url in enumerate(list(article_urls)[:limit], 1):
        log.info(f"  [{i}] {url[:80]}")
        article = fetch_article(url=url, source_name=source_name)
        if article:
            articles.append(article)
        time.sleep(random.uniform(REQUEST_DELAY_MIN, REQUEST_DELAY_MAX))

    return articles

# ─── Dataset Save ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def save_dataset(articles: list, output_path: str):
    """Save articles to JSON, appending to existing data if file exists."""
    path = Path(output_path)
    
    existing = []
    if path.exists():
        with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            existing = json.load(f)
        log.info(f"  Loaded {len(existing)} existing articles")

    # Deduplicate by URL
    existing_urls = {a["url"] for a in existing}
    new_articles = [a for a in articles if a["url"] not in existing_urls]

    combined = existing + new_articles

    with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        json.dump(combined, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False, default=str)

    log.info(f"💾 Saved {len(new_articles)} new articles (total: {len(combined)}) → {output_path}")
    return len(new_articles)

# ─── Stats Printer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def print_stats(articles: list):
    """Print a summary of collected data quality."""
    if not articles:
        print("\n⚠ No articles collected.")
        return

    authors = [a["author"] for a in articles]
    known = [a for a in authors if a != "Unknown"]
    
    # Author frequency
    from collections import Counter
    author_counts = Counter(known).most_common(10)

    print("\n" + "="*55)
    print(f"📊 COLLECTION SUMMARY")
    print("="*55)
    print(f"  Total articles:     {len(articles)}")
    print(f"  Author found:       {len(known)} ({100*len(known)//len(articles)}%)")
    print(f"  Author unknown:     {len(articles)-len(known)}")
    print(f"  Unique authors:     {len(set(known))}")
    
    sources = Counter(a["source"] for a in articles)
    print(f"\n  By source:")
    for src, count in sources.most_common():
        print(f"    {src:<25} {count}")

    print(f"\n  Top authors:")
    for author, count in author_counts:
        print(f"    {author:<30} {count} articles")
    print("="*55)

# ─── CLI Entry Point ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Indian News Dataset Scraper")
    parser.add_argument("--source",     default="all",    help="Source: all, toi, ht, ndtv, hindu, indiatoday, cricbuzz")
    parser.add_argument("--category",   default="all",    help="Category: all, india, world, sports, cricket, business, technology, entertainment")
    parser.add_argument("--limit",      type=int, default=20, help="Max articles per RSS feed (0 = no limit)")
    parser.add_argument("--output",     default="dataset.json", help="Output JSON file path")
    parser.add_argument("--author-url", default=None,     help="Scrape specific author page URL")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    all_articles = []

    # Author page mode
    if args.author_url:
        source_name = "Custom"
        for src_key, src_data in RSS_FEEDS.items():
            if src_key in args.author_url:
                source_name = src_data["source_name"]
                break
        articles = scrape_author_page(args.author_url, source_name, limit=args.limit or 50)
        all_articles.extend(articles)

    else:
        # RSS feed mode
        sources_to_run = RSS_FEEDS if args.source == "all" else {
            args.source: RSS_FEEDS[args.source]
        } if args.source in RSS_FEEDS else {}

        if not sources_to_run:
            log.error(f"Unknown source: {args.source}. Choose from: {', '.join(RSS_FEEDS.keys())}")
            return

        for src_key, src_data in sources_to_run.items():
            source_name = src_data["source_name"]
            feeds = src_data["feeds"]

            if args.category != "all":
                feeds = {args.category: feeds[args.category]} if args.category in feeds else {}

            for category, rss_url in feeds.items():
                articles = process_rss_feed(
                    rss_url=rss_url,
                    source_name=source_name,
                    category=category,
                    limit=args.limit,
                )
                all_articles.extend(articles)

    print_stats(all_articles)
    save_dataset(all_articles, args.output)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

{"returncode":0,"stdout":"-"""
Author Discovery Script
========================
Discovers real, verified author profile URLs by:
  1. Pulling articles from RSS feeds (per topic, per source)
  2. Visiting each article and extracting the author profile link from the HTML byline
  3. Saving a clean author registry: { source → topic → [{ name, profile_url }] }

This avoids ALL hardcoded IDs. Every URL in the output is extracted from live HTML.

Run:
    python discover_authors.py                  # Discover from all sources
    python discover_authors.py --source toi     # Only Times of India
    python discover_authors.py --articles 20    # Check 20 articles per feed (default: 15)
    python discover_authors.py --out authors.json
"""

import feedparser
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import json
import time
import random
import argparse
import logging
from urllib.parse import urlparse

# ─── Logging ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
logging.basicConfig(
    level=logging.INFO,
    format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s",
    datefmt="%H:%M:%S"
)
log = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# ─── Request Headers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
HEADERS = {
    "User-Agent": (
        "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) "
        "AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) "
        "Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36"
    ),
    "Accept-Language": "en-US,en;q=0.9",
    "Accept": "text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8",
    "Referer": "https://www.google.com/",
}

DELAY_MIN = 2.5
DELAY_MAX = 5.5

# ─── RSS Feed Registry (Source → Topic → RSS URL) ─────────────────────────────
#
# Each source has a dict of topic → RSS feed URL.
# We extract authors from articles in each topic feed,
# then tag those authors with that topic.
#
RSS_FEEDS = {
    "Times of India": {
        "base_url": "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com",
        "feeds": {
            "Cricket":        "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/54829575.cms",
            "Sports":         "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/4719148.cms",
            "Politics":       "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/-2128936835.cms",
            "Business":       "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/1898055.cms",
            "Technology":     "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/66949542.cms",
            "Entertainment":  "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/1081479906.cms",
            "World":          "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/296589292.cms",
            "Science":        "https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/rssfeeds/71233785.cms",
        },
        # Patterns that indicate an author profile link in the HTML
        "author_url_patterns": ["/toireporter/", "/author/"],
    },
    "Hindustan Times": {
        "base_url": "https://www.hindustantimes.com",
        "feeds": {
            "Cricket":        "https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/cricket/rssfeed.xml",
            "Sports":         "https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/sports/rssfeed.xml",
            "Politics":       "https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/india-news/rssfeed.xml",
            "Business":       "https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/business/rssfeed.xml",
            "Technology":     "https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/technology/rssfeed.xml",
            "Entertainment":  "https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/entertainment/rssfeed.xml",
            "World":          "https://www.hindustantimes.com/feeds/rss/world-news/rssfeed.xml",
        },
        "author_url_patterns": ["/author/", "/profile/"],
    },
    "NDTV": {
        "base_url": "https://www.ndtv.com",
        "feeds": {
            "Cricket":        "https://feeds.feedburner.com/CricketNDTV",
            "Sports":         "https://feeds.feedburner.com/ndtvnews-sports",
            "Politics":       "https://feeds.feedburner.com/ndtvnews-india-news",
            "Business":       "https://feeds.feedburner.com/ndtvnews-business",
            "Technology":     "https://feeds.feedburner.com/ndtvnews-tech",
            "Entertainment":  "https://feeds.feedburner.com/ndtvnews-entertainment",
            "World":          "https://feeds.feedburner.com/ndtvnews-world-news",
        },
        "author_url_patterns": ["/journalists/", "/author/", "/profile/"],
    },
    "The Hindu": {
        "base_url": "https://www.thehindu.com",
        "feeds": {
            "Cricket":        "https://www.thehindu.com/sport/cricket/?service=rss",
            "Sports":         "https://www.thehindu.com/sport/?service=rss",
            "Politics":       "https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/?service=rss",
            "Business":       "https://www.thehindu.com/business/?service=rss",
            "Technology":     "https://www.thehindu.com/sci-tech/?service=rss",
            "Entertainment":  "https://www.thehindu.com/entertainment/?service=rss",
            "World":          "https://www.thehindu.com/news/international/?service=rss",
        },
        "author_url_patterns": ["/profile/", "/author/", "/journalist/"],
    },
    "India Today": {
        "base_url": "https://www.indiatoday.in",
        "feeds": {
            "Cricket":        "https://www.indiatoday.in/rss/1206579",
            "Politics":       "https://www.indiatoday.in/rss/1206578",
            "Business":       "https://www.indiatoday.in/rss/1206580",
            "Technology":     "https://www.indiatoday.in/rss/1206581",
            "Entertainment":  "https://www.indiatoday.in/rss/1206582",
        },
        "author_url_patterns": ["/author/", "/journalist/", "/profile/"],
    },
    "Cricbuzz": {
        "base_url": "https://www.cricbuzz.com",
        "feeds": {
            "Cricket":        "https://www.cricbuzz.com/cricket-news/rss-feed",
        },
        "author_url_patterns": ["/author/", "/profile/", "/journalist/"],
    },
}

# ─── Core: Extract Author Link from Article HTML ───────────────────────────────

def extract_author_from_article(article_url: str, author_patterns: list, base_url: str) -> dict | None:
    """
    Visit a single article page and find the author's profile URL in the byline.
    Uses the source-specific URL patterns to identify the correct link.

    Returns: { "name": str, "profile_url": str } or None
    """
    try:
        resp = requests.get(article_url, headers=HEADERS, timeout=10)
        if resp.status_code != 200:
            return None

        soup = BeautifulSoup(resp.content, "html.parser")

        # Strategy 1: Find anchor tags whose href matches known author URL patterns
        for a_tag in soup.find_all("a", href=True):
            href = a_tag["href"].strip()

            # Check if this link matches any author URL pattern for this source
            if any(pattern in href for pattern in author_patterns):

                # Skip generic section links (e.g. /author/all, /toireporter/index)
                if any(skip in href.lower() for skip in ["index", "/all", "list", "search"]):
                    continue

                # Build full URL if relative
                if href.startswith("/"):
                    href = base_url.rstrip("/") + href
                elif not href.startswith("http"):
                    continue

                name = a_tag.get_text(strip=True)

                # Validate: name must look like a real person (2-60 chars, no HTML noise)
                if name and 2 < len(name) < 60 and "<" not in name and name.lower() not in [
                    "toi", "ht", "ndtv", "author", "reporter", "journalist", "staff", "desk",
                    "bureau", "agency", "correspondent", "editor", "team", "admin"
                ]:
                    return {"name": name, "profile_url": href}

        # Strategy 2: Check JSON-LD structured data for author info
        for script in soup.find_all("script", type="application/ld+json"):
            try:
                data = json.loads(script.string or "")
                items = data if isinstance(data, list) else [data]
                for item in items:
                    author_field = item.get("author")
                    if not author_field:
                        continue
                    if isinstance(author_field, dict):
                        name = author_field.get("name", "")
                        url  = author_field.get("url", "")
                        if name and url and any(p in url for p in author_patterns):
                            return {"name": name, "profile_url": url}
                    elif isinstance(author_field, list) and author_field:
                        first = author_field[0]
                        if isinstance(first, dict):
                            name = first.get("name", "")
                            url  = first.get("url", "")
                            if name and url and any(p in url for p in author_patterns):
                                return {"name": name, "profile_url": url}
            except (json.JSONDecodeError, AttributeError):
                continue

        # Strategy 3: Check meta tags
        for attr, val in [("name", "author"), ("property", "article:author")]:
            tag = soup.find("meta", attrs={attr: val})
            if tag and tag.get("content"):
                name = tag["content"].strip()
                if 2 < len(name) < 60:
                    # We have name but not profile URL — return name-only record
                    return {"name": name, "profile_url": None}

    except requests.exceptions.Timeout:
        log.debug(f"  Timeout: {article_url}")
    except Exception as e:
        log.debug(f"  Error extracting author from {article_url}: {e}")

    return None

# ─── Per-Feed Discovery ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def discover_authors_from_feed(source_name: str, topic: str, rss_url: str,
                                author_patterns: list, base_url: str,
                                articles_to_check: int = 15) -> list:
    """
    Parse an RSS feed, visit each article, extract author profile URLs.
    Returns list of { name, profile_url, topic, source }
    """
    log.info(f"  📡 [{source_name}] {topic}")
    log.info(f"     RSS: {rss_url}")

    try:
        feed = feedparser.parse(rss_url)
    except Exception as e:
        log.warning(f"  ✗ Failed to parse RSS: {e}")
        return []

    entries = feed.entries[:articles_to_check]
    if not entries:
        log.warning(f"  ✗ No entries in feed")
        return []

    log.info(f"     Articles to check: {len(entries)}")

    # Use profile_url as key to deduplicate authors found in this feed
    found = {}  # profile_url → { name, profile_url }

    for i, entry in enumerate(entries, 1):
        article_url = entry.get("link", "")
        if not article_url:
            continue

        log.info(f"     [{i}/{len(entries)}] {entry.get('title', '')[:50]}")
        author = extract_author_from_article(article_url, author_patterns, base_url)

        if author:
            key = author["profile_url"] or author["name"]
            if key not in found:
                found[key] = author
                log.info(f"     ✓ Found: {author['name']} → {author['profile_url'] or '(no profile URL)'}")
        else:
            log.info(f"     - No author link found")

        time.sleep(random.uniform(DELAY_MIN, DELAY_MAX))

    results = []
    for author in found.values():
        results.append({
            "name":        author["name"],
            "profile_url": author["profile_url"],
            "topic":       topic,
            "source":      source_name,
        })

    log.info(f"     → {len(results)} unique authors found in this feed")
    return results

# ─── Merge & Deduplicate Across Topics ───────────────────────────────────────

def merge_authors(all_discovered: list) -> dict:
    """
    Merge author records across topics.
    An author can appear in multiple topics — we keep all topic associations.
    Structure: { source → { profile_url → { name, profile_url, topics: [] } } }
    """
    registry = {}  # source → profile_url → record

    for record in all_discovered:
        source = record["source"]
        key    = record["profile_url"] or record["name"]  # fallback to name if no URL
        topic  = record["topic"]

        if source not in registry:
            registry[source] = {}

        if key not in registry[source]:
            registry[source][key] = {
                "name":        record["name"],
                "profile_url": record["profile_url"],
                "topics":      [topic],
                "source":      source,
            }
        else:
            # Author already seen — add topic if new
            if topic not in registry[source][key]["topics"]:
                registry[source][key]["topics"].append(topic)

    # Convert to clean output format
    output = {}
    for source, authors in registry.items():
        output[source] = sorted(
            list(authors.values()),
            key=lambda x: x["name"]
        )

    return output

# ─── Save ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def save_registry(registry: dict, output_path: str):
    with open(output_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        json.dump(registry, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
    log.info(f"\n💾 Saved author registry → {output_path}")

# ─── Stats ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def print_stats(registry: dict):
    print("\n" + "="*60)
    print("📊 AUTHOR DISCOVERY SUMMARY")
    print("="*60)

    total = 0
    for source, authors in registry.items():
        with_url    = [a for a in authors if a["profile_url"]]
        without_url = [a for a in authors if not a["profile_url"]]
        total += len(authors)

        print(f"\n  {source}")
        print(f"    Total authors found:  {len(authors)}")
        print(f"    With profile URL:     {len(with_url)}  ← usable for article collection")
        print(f"    Without profile URL:  {len(without_url)}  ← name only, need manual lookup")

        # Show topic breakdown
        from collections import Counter
        topic_count = Counter(t for a in authors for t in a["topics"])
        print(f"    Topics:")
        for topic, count in sorted(topic_count.items()):
            print(f"      {topic:<20} {count} authors")

    print(f"\n  TOTAL AUTHORS ACROSS ALL SOURCES: {total}")
    print("="*60)
    print("\n  Next step: Run toi_author_scraper.py with this registry")
    print("  to collect articles from each discovered author.\n")

# ─── CLI ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Discover real author profile URLs from RSS feeds")
    parser.add_argument("--source",   default="all", help="Source key or 'all': toi, ht, ndtv, hindu, indiatoday, cricbuzz")
    parser.add_argument("--topic",    default="all", help="Topic or 'all': Cricket, Sports, Politics, Business, Technology, Entertainment, World")
    parser.add_argument("--articles", type=int, default=15, help="Articles to check per feed (default: 15)")
    parser.add_argument("--out",      default="author_registry.json", help="Output JSON file")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    # Map CLI source keys to RSS_FEEDS keys
    source_key_map = {
        "toi":        "Times of India",
        "ht":         "Hindustan Times",
        "ndtv":       "NDTV",
        "hindu":      "The Hindu",
        "indiatoday": "India Today",
        "cricbuzz":   "Cricbuzz",
    }

    # Select sources to run
    if args.source == "all":
        sources_to_run = RSS_FEEDS
    else:
        key = source_key_map.get(args.source, args.source)
        if key not in RSS_FEEDS:
            log.error(f"Unknown source: {args.source}")
            log.error(f"Valid options: all, {', '.join(source_key_map.keys())}")
            return
        sources_to_run = {key: RSS_FEEDS[key]}

    all_discovered = []

    for source_name, source_config in sources_to_run.items():
        feeds        = source_config["feeds"]
        base_url     = source_config["base_url"]
        patterns     = source_config["author_url_patterns"]

        # Filter topics if requested
        if args.topic != "all":
            feeds = {args.topic: feeds[args.topic]} if args.topic in feeds else {}
            if not feeds:
                log.warning(f"Topic '{args.topic}' not found for {source_name}")
                continue

        print(f"\n{'='*60}")
        print(f"SOURCE: {source_name}  ({len(feeds)} topics)")
        print(f"{'='*60}")

        for topic, rss_url in feeds.items():
            discovered = discover_authors_from_feed(
                source_name    = source_name,
                topic          = topic,
                rss_url        = rss_url,
                author_patterns= patterns,
                base_url       = base_url,
                articles_to_check = args.articles,
            )
            all_discovered.extend(discovered)

    if not all_discovered:
        log.error("No authors discovered. Check your RSS feeds or try --articles 25")
        return

    registry = merge_authors(all_discovered)
    print_stats(registry)
    save_registry(registry, args.out)

    # Also save a flat list for easy use with toi_author_scraper.py
    flat_path = args.out.replace(".json", "_flat.json")
    flat = [a for authors in registry.values() for a in authors if a["profile_url"]]
    with open(flat_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        json.dump(flat, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
    log.info(f"💾 Flat list (profile URLs only) → {flat_path}")
    log.info(f"   {len(flat)} authors with verified profile URLs")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

  3. Saving a clean author registry: { source → topic → [{ name, profile_url }] }

import feedparser

# ─── Logging ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
